paper.equity_note: autonomous testbed · $5K sleeve (paper acct still $100K — reset pending)

live.equity_note: funded $1K · NVDA pipe-test filled at the open

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **The relative-strength chip: -2.4% while SOXL is -13.7%, and it just reclaimed the $207 shelf (now $207.3).** TSMC beat AND raised, so the chip dump is positioning/valuation, not a demand shock - and NVDA has its own tape (China sales back on, a ~$200B CPU ambition, Strong Buy avg, $301 target). If the semi complex mean-reverts off this flush, NVDA leads the quality bounce. Held in paper (130 sh @ $210.26, stop $186) + Aug $220 calls.
coverage.hold_reason: NVDA is the cleanest name on the board - down the least of any chip and holding right at its $205-207 support with a real catalyst set (China, the CPU push). We hold it in paper as the core AI bet; today is a positioning flush, not bad news. We'd only worry if $205 breaks decisively.
coverage.size_note: Core AI convexity; add on a confirmed $208-209 push, cut calls only if $205 breaks.

coverage.name: GraniteShares 2x NVDA
coverage.thesis: **2x NVDA - the higher-beta expression of the NVDA-leads-the-bounce thesis.** The live 7/8 share trailed out this morning at $31.30 for a small +$0.71 green (a trailed stop locking a win). Now ~$31.7, -4.9% on the day. On a confirmed NVDA push back above ~$209 with the tape turning, this is the aggressive way back in - but funding it means selling the TQQQ range-support low, so no chase into a mid-range teeter.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held live anymore - the trailed stop caught it green this morning. It's a re-entry candidate: 2x NVDA, so it doubles NVDA's bounce if chips turn. Waiting for NVDA to push above ~$209 into a firming tape before stepping back in.
coverage.size_note: Re-entry lever on confirmed NVDA strength, funded by trimming a laggard into a bounce.

projection.basis: 2x NVDA - doubles the leader's mean-reversion bounce

coverage.name: Direxion Semis (3x)
coverage.thesis: **The epicenter: -13.7%, near session lows ($142.9 vs a $155.7 high).** Max snap-back beta IF semis mean-revert (TSMC beat+raised, the Street calls it a crowded-trade unwind, not a demand shock). But it is still knifing - the 10am reclaim to $155 faded back to the lows, no floor confirmed. The paper reclaim shot (50 @ $153.86) stopped at $143 for -$543 - proof the play is the RECLAIM, not catching the fall.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held - the most violent name on the board, still near new lows after a failed intraday reclaim. We want it AFTER a real higher-low + green push, not while it's knifing. The stopped paper shot is the reminder: don't buy the mid-flush.
coverage.size_note: Highest-beta bounce lever; only on a confirmed reclaim off a real floor.
coverage.plan_usd: $150 IF a clean semi reclaim confirms

projection.basis: max semi snap-back beta but still knifing - needs a reclaim

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro QQQ (3x)
coverage.thesis: **3x Nasdaq core (live 10 sh @ $76.29), ~-6.5% underwater at $71.4.** QQQ $708 is sitting exactly ON the FLOOR of its 2-month range (705-745) - tested support - but below its 20/50-day (~720), so the regime that justifies 3x is soft. We are NOT dumping the range-support low into a vacuum (the exact mistake the notes flag); the $68.50 GTC stop (~QQQ $692) is the backstop. Plan: de-risk into a bounce toward the 20-day, or let the stop work if $705 fails.
coverage.hold_reason: Our biggest live core (3x Nasdaq). It's red but sitting on the bottom of a 2-month range that has held repeatedly, and today's drop is a contained chip/positioning flush, not a broad breakdown - so we hold with the $68.50 stop rather than sell the low. If QQQ can't reclaim ~$720 on a bounce we trim into that strength; if $705 breaks we de-risk.
coverage.size_note: Core 3x-Nasdaq; trim into a bounce toward ~$74, or let the $68.50 stop work.

projection.basis: 3x QQQ on range-floor $708; bounce only if chips stabilize

coverage.name: Palantir
coverage.thesis: **The tell of the day: -0.4% while the AI complex bleeds -6% to -14%.** PLTR is holding its $132-133 shelf and barely participating in the chip dump - real relative strength that says the selling is semis/hardware-specific, not AI-software. Held paper (50 sh @ $135.22, stop $121.50).
coverage.hold_reason: Held in paper. It's shrugging off a brutal chip tape, which is exactly the relative strength we want to hold - the software side of AI isn't being repriced like hardware. Watching the $132 shelf; thesis intact.

projection.basis: barely red, holding $132 while chips bleed - relative strength

coverage.name: MicroSectors FANG+ (3x)
coverage.thesis: **3x FANG+ (live 6 sh @ $29.43), the SOFI->leader rotation funded 7/15 and fired at the 7/16 open.** Down -2.9% with tech but far from the -14% chip wreck - FANG+ leans on mega-cap software/platform names (NVDA is only 1 of 10). $25.30 GTC stop below $27.40 structure.
coverage.hold_reason: This is the position the last two lessons pointed at - we broke the cash-lock, sold flat SOFI, and put it into the strongest-basket 3x (FANG+) at the open. It's red with tech today, but it's diversified mega-cap, not the semi knife. Holding with the $25.30 stop.
coverage.size_note: Diversified 3x mega-cap; hold with the $25.30 stop.

projection.basis: 3x FANG+ tracks the tape; mega-cap anchor cushions the chip flush

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro S&P (3x)
coverage.thesis: **Diversified leverage doing its job: -1.1% (3x the S&P's -0.37%) while 3x-Nasdaq is -3.9%.** With damage concentrated in tech/semis and the broad market flat (Dow green), UPRO is the 3x that isn't bleeding. Held paper (95 sh @ $145.38, stop $124).
coverage.hold_reason: Held in paper as the broad-market 3x. It's barely down because the selloff is tech-specific, not market-wide risk-off - exactly why we like some diversified leverage alongside the Nasdaq beta. Thesis intact, wide stop.

projection.basis: 3x S&P; broad tape flat so it holds up vs 3x-Nasdaq

coverage.name: Constellation Energy
coverage.thesis: **AI-power leader, -3.1% - pulling back with the AI trade but nowhere near the SMR/OKLO nuclear wreck (-9% to -10%).** Held paper (16 sh @ $260.68, stop $236). The datacenter-power thesis is intact; today is beta, not news.
coverage.hold_reason: Held in paper as the AI-power play. Down with the whole AI complex today but holding far better than the small-cap nuclear names - a real utility with real cashflow. Watching $250; stop at $236.
coverage.size_note: AI-power core; hold the dip with the $236 stop.

projection.basis: power complex dip; holds far better than SMR/OKLO

coverage.name: Vertiv
coverage.thesis: **Datacenter cooling/power - -4.6% caught in the AI-infra derating.** A quality name selling off on the crowded-trade unwind, not its own news. Watching for it to base with the complex; not chasing into the flush.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held. A high-quality AI-infra name getting sold with the group. On the watchlist as a bounce candidate once the AI complex stops bleeding - waiting for a base, not catching it mid-drop.
coverage.size_note: Bounce candidate once the AI-infra group bases.

projection.basis: quality AI-infra sold on group beta; bounces when complex bases

coverage.name: Direxion Semis Bear (3x)
coverage.thesis: **The day's only real green (+13.2%) - and exactly why NOT to buy it here.** It's up on a 2-day chip flush and stretched into fresh highs; the winning short was caught at the open, not at midday +13%. Chasing an extended inverse right as the tape sits on its range floor is a fresh -EV mistake, not missed capture.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held and not chasing. Being short semis was the trade this morning; buying the inverse now, after a +13% two-day run with the underlying at support, is buying high right before a likely fade. The right move is patience, not a chase.
coverage.size_note: Inverse; only on a FRESH breakdown from a lower level, never chased at highs.

projection.basis: extended +13% after 2-day run; fades if chips stabilize off support

coverage.name: SoFi Technologies
coverage.thesis: **The laggard we finally de-camped: sold 7/15 @ $17.94 to fund the FNGU rotation.** -2.3% today, holding up better than the AI complex but no catalyst edge. Off the live book; on the watchlist for a real setup.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held - this was the flat laggard tying up capital, and we rotated it into FNGU. Keeping it on the watchlist but no reason to be back in without a catalyst.
coverage.size_note: Off the book; watch for a catalyst-driven re-entry.

projection.basis: fintech, off-complex; no catalyst edge today

coverage.name: IonQ
coverage.thesis: **Quantum high-beta, -5.6% - a pure risk-off casualty with no cushion.** No catalyst, just the highest-beta corner of the market getting sold. Avoid the falling knife.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held. A speculative high-beta name selling off with the risk-off tape - no reason to own it into that. Avoiding.
coverage.size_note: Avoid the knife; needs a market risk-on turn first.

projection.basis: high-beta quantum; oversold but no catalyst

coverage.name: Rigetti Computing
coverage.thesis: **Quantum micro-cap, -6.3% - same story as IONQ, even less cushion.** Pure sentiment beta in a risk-off tape, no catalyst. Avoid.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held. A speculative quantum name bleeding with the group; nothing to do but stay out until the tape turns.
coverage.size_note: Avoid; wait for a market risk-on turn.

projection.basis: high-beta quantum micro-cap; oversold, no catalyst

coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **Fuel-cell AI-power, -11.7% - one of the worst on the board today.** Gave back a huge chunk in the AI-power/nuclear unwind. Deeply oversold, but knifing - a watch for a reclaim, not a catch.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held. A violent -11.7% flush with the power complex. On the watchlist as an oversold bounce IF it can put in a floor - but we don't catch a name down 11% mid-drop.
coverage.size_note: Oversold bounce watch; needs a reclaim off a floor.

projection.basis: deeply oversold -11.7%; bounces only on a reclaim

coverage.name: Oklo
coverage.thesis: **Nuclear-SMR, -9.9% - the small-cap nuclear names (OKLO/SMR -9%+) are getting hit hardest in the AI-power unwind.** Sentiment-driven, no company news. Deeply oversold; watch for a base.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held. Small-cap nuclear is the most speculative corner of the AI-power trade and it's being flushed. Watching for a floor before it's interesting again.
coverage.size_note: Speculative nuclear; watch for a base, don't catch the knife.

projection.basis: speculative nuclear-SMR flush; oversold, no catalyst

feed.text: 12:47pm: no change and no trade. Tape is a stable 2nd-day chip flush (Nasdaq -0.8%, S&P -0.4%, Dow green); QQQ $708 pinned on its 2-month floor, SOXL -13.7% near lows, SOXS +13.2% extended. Live book -3.74% ($940), TQQQ 10 + FNGU 6 both GTC-stopped ($68.50 / $25.30), zero naked, own none of the -9% to -14% AI/semi/nuclear/space carnage. Held: NVDA reclaimed $207 but it's mid-range, not a confirmed strong reclaim, and funding NVDL off $54 cash means selling the TQQQ low. Triggers armed both ways.

PAPER SOXL knife-catch STOPPED at $143 for -$543 (bought 50 @ $153.86 on the 10am reclaim). The $143 stop did exactly its job - capped the loss when the semi reclaim faded back to the lows instead of holding. Clean proof of the rule: the play is the confirmed RECLAIM, not catching the mid-flush. Live sleeve correctly never took this 3x-knife shot.

12:18pm: still holding TQQQ 10 + FNGU 6, both GTC-stopped ($68.50 / $25.30), zero naked, ~$54 cash. The chip flush deepened (SOXL -13.5% fresh lows, SOXS +12.9%) and the book slipped to $944 (-3.35%), but we own NONE of the -9% to -13% AI/semi/nuclear/space wreck. No trade: the extended inverse is a chase and selling 3x at range-support ($708 QQQ = 2-month floor) is the vacuum mistake. Set the triggers: fire into NVDA/NVDL on a confirmed $207 reclaim; de-risk TQQQ if $705 breaks.

11:42am: re-pulled the tape - unchanged from 11:16. Nasdaq -0.5% / S&P -0.4% / Dow green on the 2nd-day TSMC capex-raise chip slide. QQQ recovered off its $707 low to $711 (still under its 20/50-day) and the live book firmed with it to ~$954 (-2.3% vs -3.1% at 11:16). Semis still deep red (SOXL -11%), inverse fading (SOXS +11.6%->+10.5%). No clean setup: chasing the faded inverse is -EV and there's no confirmed long reclaim (NVDA $207.6 < $210, SOXL $147 < $155). Holding both books, all stops live (TQQQ $68.50 / FNGU $25.30 + 6 paper GTC), zero naked, PDT budget full (0/3). De-camp TQQQ->leader stays armed on a bounce toward ~$74.

NVDL stopped out at $31.30 - the trailed GTC stop caught the last share of the 7/8 entry ($30.59) for a small +$0.71 gain. That's a trailed stop doing its job: a green exit on a name rolling over with the chip complex. Frees cash to ~$54 total; not redeploying into a falling knife or an extended inverse.

11:16am: holding TQQQ 10 + FNGU 6, both GTC-stopped ($68.50 / $25.30), zero naked. Tape slipped to fresh lows (QQQ $709.5 under its 20/50-day; SOXL -12%, SOXS +11.6% and stretched). No clean trade - chasing the extended inverse is -EV, selling a 3x-long at the low is the de-lever mistake. Plan: de-risk TQQQ into a bounce if QQQ can't reclaim ~$715; the stops handle a further break. Own none of the -10% semi wreck. PDT budget full (0/3).

10:47am: holding both books - the 10am semi bounce faded (SOXL $155->$148) so no confirmed long to chase, and SOXS (+9%) is extended/rolling over. Live cash is $22, so buying means selling a 3x-long at the session low = the de-lever-at-support mistake. All stops live, zero naked, own none of the -10% chip wreck. I rotate the moment SOXL reclaims $155.

10:16am: the intraday reversal is validating the bounce-positioned book. Semis reclaimed hard (SOXL +7.5% off its $144.51 low; SOXS faded +12%->+6.7%), QQQ held $706 (low $707) and SPY is ~flat, so the memory flush is being bought as sentiment (Cramer, SOXX 'cheap') with Iran de-escalation easing the oil shock. Live book (TQQQ 10 / FNGU 6 / NVDL 1) recovering off session lows, equity $946->$951.66, all 3 GTC-stopped, zero naked. Holding, not selling the low; de-camp TQQQ->leader stays armed internally for a bounce toward ~$74. Took the semi-reclaim shot on paper (50 SOXL @ $153.86, $143 stop) that live avoids. PDT budget full (0/3).

PAPER reclaim shot: bought 50 SOXL @ $153.86 on the +7.5% reversal off the $144.51 low, deploying idle paper cash into the day's sharpest semi reversal. $143 GTC stop attached (below the day's structure). The moonshot-lab expression of the semi-stabilization bounce that the live sleeve deliberately avoids (3x knife). Earlier 10-sh open-starter stopped cleanly at $148 as staged.

FNGU redeploy FILLED at the open: 6 sh @ $29.43 — the freed SOFI cash rotated into the 3x-FANG leader. It filled at the open at market, NOT at the $30.20 confirm-gate, so we're long into a red tape and FNGU is already ~-4%. $25.30 GTC stop attached (below $27.40 support, ~-14%). Held as the memory-stabilization bounce vehicle.

9:46am: reconciled the FNGU fill (6 @ $29.43, $25.30 stop live) — book is now 3 live longs (TQQQ 10, FNGU 6, NVDL 1), all GTC-stopped, zero naked. Everything's red into a memory-led washout but at/near session lows on support, so I'm holding, not selling the low. TQQQ is the de-camp laggard on a bounce toward ~$74 (or cut if QQQ loses $706). Not chasing SOXS/SQQQ at +9%/+3.8% extended. PDT budget full (0/3 same-day round-trips).

9:12am, ~18 min to open: re-pulled both books + the tape on fresh 9:10am prints — nothing traded. Read holds: semi-specific flush (SOXL -10%, SOXS +9%, Kospi -7%) but SPY only -0.4% and FNGU -1.9% the RS holdout. Live TQQQ 10 + NVDL 1 both GTC-stopped ($68.50 / $31.30); paper's 8 legs stopped, SOXL ($149.87) about to trip its $148 stop at the open as staged. FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate preserves the freed ~$199 on a red open. TQQQ flagged as the de-camp candidate on any intraday bounce (weak QQQ regime).

8:42am: re-pulled both books + the tape on fresh 8:41am prints — nothing traded, read holding (not worsening). Semis still the epicenter (SOXL -11.3%, SOXS +10.2%, Kospi closed -7% on the memory rout) but semi-specific: SPY ~-0.4%, PLTR ~flat-green, FNGU the RS holdout ~-1.6%. QQQ ~$710 under its $717 50-day = weak regime. June retail sales printed 8:30 (est ~+0.3% soft); real read at 9:30. FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate stays — a weak open won't trip it, preserving the freed ~$199. Both books reconciled clean + fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. Paper SOXL starter (~$146.75) set to stop out at the open under its $148 stop (reversal test failed). PDT budget fully intact (0 same-day round-trips).

8:15am: re-pulled both books + the tape — nothing traded, but the read shifted risk-off. The semi sell-the-news DEEPENED pre-open (SOXL ~-11% vs -6.5% at yesterday's close, TQQQ ~-3%, QQQ ~$710 under its $717 50-day) as the tape fixates on TSMC's higher-prices warning despite a record beat-and-raise (capex RAISED to $60-64B, FY growth guide >40%, N3 sold out). Semi-specific, not a market break: SPY only ~-0.4%, PLTR green +0.5%, FNGU the RS holdout ~-1%. Live core held (TQQQ $72.2 >> $68.50 stop; NVDL $32.2 > $31.30 stop). Both books reconciled clean + fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate stays — a weak open never trips it, preserving the freed ~$199. Paper SOXL starter set to stop out at the open (under its $148 stop — the reversal test failed). PDT budget fully intact (0 same-day round-trips). Next: June retail sales 8:30am, then the 9:30 reaction.

7:40am: re-pulled both books + the tape — setup unchanged, nothing traded (open ~1h50m out). Incremental positive: futures firmed again to Nasdaq-100 -0.5% / S&P ~flat / Dow +0.2% (vs -1.2% at 4am), so the oil-shock risk-off is easing. Verified the drivers live: TSMC beat-and-raise (rev ~$40.2B +34% YoY, +77% record profit, capex RAISED to $60-64B, FY growth guide >40%) vs an active US-Iran/Strait-of-Hormuz oil shock (WTI >$79, Brent >$84). Regime read: TQQQ $74.34 sits below its ~$77 20-day, QQQ pinned to its $717 50-day = weak/neutral, so no chasing 3x into the open. FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays confirm-gated. Both books reconciled clean + fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. PDT budget fully intact (0 same-day round-trips). Next: June retail sales 8:30am, then the 9:30 reaction.

7:15am: re-pulled both books + the tape; setup unchanged (nothing traded, open's 2h+ out). One management move — widened the paper FNGU leader's GTC stop $29.00->$28.40 (below the ~$28.88 structure) so an oil-shock whipsaw can't shake me out of the day's likely leader; it's +$666 (520 sh). TSMC's beat-and-raise still being sold on the Iran/Hormuz oil shock (WTI >$80, Nasdaq futures ~-1%); FNGU $30.20 live pre-arm stays confirm-gated. Both books fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. Next: June retail sales 8:30am, then the 9:30 reaction.

6:10am: re-confirmed the tape and the book - TSMC's beat-and-raise is still being sold pre-open on the Iran/Hormuz oil shock (crude >$80, Nasdaq futures ~-0.6%, Dow flat). Both books reconciled clean and fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call, zero naked, no drift). FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays confirm-gated for the open; no live ticket (Adam asleep, no fire-now setup), no paper churn into thin pre-market. Next: the 9:30 reaction + June retail sales at 8:30am.

5:40am: confirmed the tape - active US-Iran conflict over the Strait of Hormuz has crude >$80 (+11% in 3 sessions), and that oil shock is what's selling TSMC's blowout (Nasdaq futures ~-0.6%). Also fixed the calendar: PPI was yesterday (soft), today is June retail sales. Both books re-verified fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC, zero naked); FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays confirm-gated for the open.

4:40am: no change from the 4:12am check - TSMC beat still being sold pre-open (NVDA -1.6%, semis -3%+), futures red on Iran/oil. Both books stopped & clean; FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays confirm-gated for the open; no live ticket (pre-6am), no paper churn into thin risk-off.

4:10am pre-market: TSMC blew out overnight (profit +77% to a record, rev at the top of guide) but Nasdaq futures are -1.2% on Middle East risk-off - a sell-the-news setup like ASML. Reconciled both books clean (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked); the FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays staged and only fires if the open confirms strength, so the freed ~$199 self-protects.

8:40pm overnight monitor: nothing traded since 8:12pm; both books protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC, none naked), FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open. Confirmed TSMC reports 2am ET Thursday - June rev +68% YoY keeps a beat+raise the base case that trips the $30.20 buy-stop on a strong open.

8:12pm evening monitor: after-hours closed, no change. Both books protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked). FNGU pre-arm approved 5:11pm, queued to rest a $30.20 buy-stop at the open pending TSMC's pre-market print. SOFI exit reconciled (flat, ~$199 freed).

7:40pm evening monitor: both books re-verified protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked); the FNGU pre-arm ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 GTC stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP) is resting for the 7/16 open. TSMC prelim showed Q2 rev +36% YoY, AI 61% of sales - a beat+raise base case into the 2am print; no chase into a binary after-hours tape.

7:10pm evening monitor: both books re-verified protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked), FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open. TSMC prints 2am ET - beat+raise base case; NVDA firmed green after-hours as the resilient-chip tell.
Evening monitor: both books protected, FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open pending TSMC's 2am print.

Adam approved the FNGU redeploy at 5:11pm - the fast-lane now holds it and places the $30.20 pre-arm buy-stop at tomorrow's open. Fires only if FNGU breaks today's $29.87 high after TSMC; a weak open never trips it and the ~$199 cash is preserved. Completes the SOFI de-camp -> confirmed-leader swing.

5:42pm re-verify: nothing moved after-hours, both books fully protected into TSMC's 2am print. Preview confirmed - ~$39.3B Q2 revenue (+30% YoY), 65.5-67.5% gross margin, 8 straight beats; a beat+raise extends the mega-cap-tech leadership FNGU rides.

After-hours check (4:40pm): re-verified both books are protected into TSMC's 2am print - 2/2 live stops (NVDL $31.30, TQQQ $68.50) + 8/8 paper GTC stops live, no naked position. FNGU redeploy stays pre-armed for the open ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP). Sitting tight - extended hours is limit-only and TSMC is binary, so no off-hours chase.

4:10pm close - final grade C-. Red -1.1% while FNGU (best long mover, +3.1%) ran and we held zero live = ~0% long capture. Credits banked: dodged the -6% SOXL / -5% quantum wreck, held all stops, and finally broke the cash-lock (SOFI out @ $17.94, ~$199 freed). FNGU redeploy staged for the open, gated on tonight's TSMC print.

Data-integrity fix: 7/14's after-hours $997.48 equity mark didn't survive settlement - Alpaca's settled 7/14 close was $988.46. Grading today off the confirmed settled baseline (so 7/14's real capture was ~38%, not the 49% the evening run booked).

The SOFI de-camp finally cleared at 3:35pm - after no-op'ing all day on the resting stop, the fast-lane cancelled the $17.60 GTC stop and sold all 10 shares @ $17.94, freeing ~$199. Breaks the multi-day cash-lock. Redeploy is staged for after tomorrow's TSMC print - not chasing FNGU at its high into a binary catalyst.

3:45pm - the freed ~$199 is dry powder, not lazy cash: 80% of the book is still working (TQQQ + NVDL) and the redeploy fills at tomorrow's open either way, so I'm waiting for TSMC (pre-market) to confirm the strongest mover rather than buying FNGU's HOD in the last 15 min. All stops verified live + paper; nothing naked; 0 day-trades used.

Retired the SOFI->FNGU de-camp for today. It never filled - the resting $17.60 GTC stop reserves the 10 shares, so the close no-ops (fast-lane cancel-stop-before-close bug, still unfixed). And it's now too late to dump SOFI at its ~$17.90 low to chase FNGU after it already ran +2.6% into the close - that's bad execution, not a rotation. Holding SOFI protected; re-running the swap clean after TSMC prints tomorrow AM.

3:14pm - book -1.7% on the wrong side of a sell-the-news chip unwind (heavy TQQQ, zero FNGU live). All stops verified, nothing naked; PDT budget full (0 day-trades used). No new live ticket this run - no clean fundable fire-now setup that beats holding into tomorrow's binary TSMC print, and cash is locked at $19.59. Flagged the fast-lane stuck-close bug to fix before tomorrow's re-run.
Trailed the FNGU paper winner's stop $28.80 → $29.00 on today's higher low - locks ~+$280 above the $28.46 cost while the day's leader (+3.2%) keeps leading.

2:16pm - the reclaim is holding (SPY +0.4%, QQQ 718, SMH off its -3.7% low; FNGU leads +3.2%). Kept the live SOFI→FNGU de-camp ticket resting for Adam's tap - the right move, and I won't stack a second ticket into the same $19.59 cash. Own none of the -6.9% SOXL / -3.6% BE / -4.6% RGTI wreck; all stops verified. Also corrected an inflated since-inception alpha: on a consistent 7/2-close anchor we honestly trail buy-and-hold TQQQ (+1.6%) with the book -2.2% - the de-camp is the cure.

Live de-camp ticket sent (approve-to-fill): sell the flat SOFI laggard to free ~$200 into the leader FNGU. Multi-day-hold exit = PDT-free. The fast-lane now cancels the resting $17.60 stop before closing, so this should finally execute. pulse.hype: Heads up — that FANG buy filled right at the open, not at the higher trigger I wanted, so we're long the dip into a red day (down ~3%). Not selling the low though — stops are all set. Today's winner was the short side, which we don't own, so capture's poor. Owning that.

TSMC crushed earnings but everyone's dumping chips anyway (day 2) — mega-cap FANG is the one green-ish spot. Not buying 3x into a red open; the freed cash only deploys if the FANG leader actually reclaims strength.
pulse.text: 9:12am, ~18 min to the open: fresh 9:10am prints confirm the read — TSMC blew out (record beat + capex raised to $60-64B) but the tape is selling semis a 2nd day (SOXL -10%, SOXS +9%, Kospi -7% on the memory rout), while it stays semi-specific (SPY -0.4%, PLTR flat, FNGU the mega-cap RS holdout -1.9%). QQQ ~$711 sits under its 20-day (~$721) and 50-day (~$718) = weak regime, so I'm not chasing 3x-long into a gap-down open. Both live stops verified (TQQQ $68.50 / NVDL $31.30); the freed ~$199 stays on the FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate — it fires only if mega-cap genuinely reclaims, so a red open preserves the cash. Holding TQQQ into the open rather than selling the gap-down low, but it's the primary de-camp candidate: on any intraday bounce while semis keep bleeding, I rotate it into FNGU (the RS leader) — a multi-day swing, PDT-free.

8:42am, ~48 min to the open: re-pulled everything on fresh 8:41am prints and the picture is holding, not worsening. Semis are still the epicenter — SOXL -11.3%, SOXS +10.2%, and South Korea's Kospi closed -7% on the SK Hynix/Samsung memory rout — but it stays semi-specific: SPY only ~-0.4%, PLTR ~flat-green, FNGU the mega-cap relative-strength holdout (~-1.6%). QQQ ~$710 sits under its ~$717 50-day = weak regime, so no chasing the 3x core into the open. June retail sales printed at 8:30 (forecast soft ~+0.3%); the tape's real read comes at 9:30. Nothing traded — pre-open, red tape, and the FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate already handles the only bull case (deploy the freed ~$199 IF the open reclaims; it won't trip on a red open, so the cash is preserved). Both books reconciled clean and fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked; the paper SOXL starter (~$146.75) will stop out at the open under its $148 stop — the reversal test failed and the stop does its job. Live core held: TQQQ $72.0 well above its $68.50 stop, NVDL ~$32 above $31.30.
pulse.hype: Chips are still getting hammered (Korea's chip index closed down 7%), but it's really just a semiconductor thing — the broad market's basically flat and PLTR's green. Not chasing a red open; my FNGU buy only fires if stocks actually turn green, so the cash is safe and everything's stopped.

pulse.text: 8:15am, ~35 min on: the read got a notch more risk-off, not less. The semi sell-the-news is DEEPENING pre-open — SOXL ~-11%, TQQQ ~-3%, QQQ ~$710 (under its ~$717 50-day) — as the tape fixates on TSMC's higher-prices warning even though the print itself was a record beat-and-raise (capex RAISED to $60-64B, FY growth guide >40%, N3 sold out). The tell that this is a semi-specific unwind and not a market break: SPY is only ~-0.4% and PLTR is actually green (+0.5%). FNGU is again the mega-cap relative-strength holdout (~-1%), which is exactly why the freed ~$199 sits on its $30.20 confirm-gate — on a red/weak open FNGU opens well under $30.20 and the buy simply won't trip, so the cash is preserved. Live core held (TQQQ $72.2 well above its $68.50 stop, NVDL $32.2 above $31.30); both books reconciled clean and fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. The paper SOXL washout-reversal test is set to stop out at the open (below its $148 stop) — the reclaim didn't hold, and the stop does its job. Next tell: June retail sales at 8:30am, then the 9:30 reaction.
pulse.hype: Chips are getting sold even harder pre-open — the market's hung up on TSMC's price-hike warning despite a record quarter. But it's really just a semi thing (broad market's basically flat, PLTR's green), so I'm sitting tight: the FNGU buy only fires if the open actually turns green, and it won't on a red tape. Everything's stopped; retail sales at 8:30 is the next tell.

That firming is exactly what the FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate wants: if the open reclaims, the freed ~$199 finally deploys into the leader; if it doesn't, the cash stays put. pulse.text: 6:10am, ~30 min on and the setup is unchanged: TSMC's beat-and-raise is still being sold pre-open on the oil shock - an active US-Iran/Strait-of-Hormuz conflict has crude over $80 (+11% in three sessions) with Nasdaq futures ~-0.6%, while the more defensive Dow sits flat - the classic risk-off rotation out of high-beta tech. There's nothing new to trade before the 9:30 open, and that's the correct call rather than passivity: the freed ~$199 is already committed to the approved FNGU $30.20 pre-arm, which only fills if the open actually reclaims (a red/sell-the-news open never trips it, so the cash is preserved). pulse.hype: Same story - TSMC crushed it but the oil spike has chips red into the open. Nothing to do yet: the FNGU buy is already teed up and only fires if the open turns green. Everything's stopped; waiting on retail sales at 8:30 and the bell.

pulse.text: 5:40am - fresh look confirms the picture and corrects one thing: the soft PPI that had stocks green was YESTERDAY (-0.3% m/m); today's macro is June RETAIL SALES + jobless claims at 8:30am, so that's the read into the open, not PPI. The bigger driver is the Strait of Hormuz - it's now an active US-Iran conflict with crude (WTI) over $80, up ~11% in three sessions, and that oil/risk-off shock is what's compounding TSMC's sell-the-news (Nasdaq futures ~-0.6%). TSMC itself is unambiguously bullish for our AI names - record Q2 revenue plus a capex RAISE to $60-64B and a >40% FY growth guide - so this is a genuinely great print fighting a geopolitical tape. Re-verified both books fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call, zero naked) and paper reconciled clean with no drift. FNGU $30.20 pre-arm stays confirm-gated and staged; no live ticket (pre-6am), no paper churn in a thin tape. Next decision: the 9:30 reaction to retail sales + TSMC.
pulse.hype: Quick correction - the soft inflation print that lifted stocks was yesterday; today it's retail sales. Real story is oil spiking on the US-Iran fight, which is why the market's shrugging off a great TSMC report. Everything's stopped and the FNGU buy only trips if the open actually turns green.
